checklist.title: Separate signal, context, and composite render outputs for secondary curvature frames
Render explicit zero-state context for identically zero fields instead of implicit black frames
Block publish-invariant-latest when citation manifest validation fails
Maintain claim-to-source mapping with uncertainty notes for hypothesis claims

claims.claimText: Observed gravitational-wave products and theoretical curvature-invariant plots must be rendered as separate lanes.
claims.uncertaintyRationale: Lane-separation policy is implementation-specific and does not imply external physical validation.
claims.scopeBoundary: This claim defines output interpretation boundaries only and does not establish transport or viability performance.

claims.claimText: NHM2 curvature invariant frames in this pipeline are secondary solve-backed visualization artifacts, not primary proof surfaces.
Future NHM2 mechanism closure may enable stronger physically interpretable volumetric curvature signatures.
claims.uncertaintyNote: Predictive projection only; not validated by present bounded-stack evidence.
claims.uncertaintyRationale: Mechanism closure remains unresolved in this repository; literature only bounds plausible directions.
claims.scopeBoundary: This claim is scenario-planning context and does not certify a physically admissible NHM2 transport regime.

claims.claimText: The NHM2 lapse alpha sweep treats centerlineAlpha as the only clocking dial while preserving route and coordinate transport schedule checks in fail-closed invariants.
claims.uncertaintyRationale: Isolation semantics are policy- and implementation-bound until all sweep profiles complete pass-level full-loop audits.
claims.scopeBoundary: This claim constrains decomposition interpretation and does not prove that any specific alpha profile is promoted.

claims.claimText: Exploratory alpha bracket entries are retained as diagnostic unless explicit promotion override is requested and full-loop gates pass.

The locked_coordinate_schedule invariant in NHM2 alpha-sweep full-loop checks must compare mission coordinate time against classical mission coordinate reference on a common target-coupled basis, while local route span remains diagnostic context only.
claims.uncertaintyRationale: Coordinate-lock interpretation depends on current comparator contracts and may change with future contract revisions.
claims.scopeBoundary: This claim defines comparator semantics only and does not by itself validate elapsed-time reductions.
